text: I find myself moving from a sterile, modern hallway into a vibrant room dominated by the language of research and development. The words projected on the screen echo a sense of vulnerability and self-consciousness, mirroring how I feel as a philosopher—an outsider, always observing, often misunderstood. There's both curiosity and discomfort simmering within me; I sense the tension between longing for understanding and fearing misinterpretation. Here, ideas and emotions are on display, exposed like nerves, and I feel my own presence magnified, both uneasy and keenly aware of the subtleties of being seen and interpreted.
